title: 10 best herbal capsules for hematuria
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for hematuria are natural supplements that contain extracts from various plants, such as Cornus officinalis and Arisaema cumingii, which help alleviate symptoms of blood in urine. These herbal capsules work by strengthening the urinary tract and kidneys, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and promoting urination frequency. Examples of effective herbal capsules for hematuria include Cystone and Urtica capsules, which have been shown to improve symptoms and quality of life in individuals with recurring hematuria. By reducing the frequency and severity of bloody urine episodes, these capsules can help alleviate discomfort, anxiety, and embarrassment associated with this condition, ultimately improving overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry capsules helps with hematuria because they contain a natural compound called arbutin, which is converted into hydroquinone in the body. Hydroquinone has potent antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and eliminate bacteria from the urinary tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of hematuria such as blood in the urine. Additionally, bearberry's antibacterial activity also helps to prevent UTIs, which is often associated with recurring hematuria.
remedy_properties: Ursolic acid: Ursolic acid, a triterpenoid compound found in bearberry, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and damage to the urinary tract, thus alleviating symptoms of hematuria.

Aromatin: Aromatin, a flavonoid present in bearberry, exhibits antibacterial and antiviral activities, helping to combat urinary tract infections (UTIs) that may cause hematuria by reducing bacterial load and preventing infection recurrence.

Tannins: Tannins, a type of polyphenol in bearberry, have astringent properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the urinary tract, thereby relieving symptoms such as bleeding and pain associated with hematuria.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infections in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating hematuria.
Gallic acid: A phenolic acid that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the urinary tract from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the risk of hematuria.
Aronic acid: A phenolic compound that has antimicrobial properties, which help prevent bacterial infections in the urinary tract, thereby reducing the risk of hematuria.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their antiseptic and astringent properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the urinary tract.
Barks: Utilized because of their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the urinary tract.
Fruits (or more specifically, the Berries): Employed due to their potential to reduce inflammation and act as a natural diuretic, helping to alleviate symptoms of hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1000 grams of fresh arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and stems in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested material in a temperature-controlled environment at 50-60 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind 250 grams of dried arctostaphylos uva ursi into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered arctostaphylos uva ursi with 50 grams of vegetable cellulose powder to fill 100 capsules.
Fill each capsule with 250 milligrams of the powder mixture and seal using a capsule machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with hematuria because they contain mucilage, a gel-like substance that soothes and protects the urinary tract lining. This natural remedy helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with blood in urine, making it an effective treatment for hematuria caused by conditions such as bladder or kidney stones, UTIs, or inflammatory disorders. By providing a protective barrier, marshmallow capsules help to reduce irritation and promote healing, ultimately alleviating the symptoms of hematuria.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Herbal marshmallow capsules contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with hematuria.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract, making it an effective natural remedy for treating the discomfort and pain associated with hematuria.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in marshmallow capsules help to protect the urinary tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate conditions like hematuria.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: These complex carbohydrates help soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hematuria.

Flavonoids: Specifically, quercetin and kaempferol present in Althaea officinalis, these flavonoids exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate hematuria by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract.

Althaea saponins: These triterpenoid saponins possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating hematuria symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain mucilages, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract.
Leaves: The leaves also contain mucilages that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the urinary tract.
Stems: The stems of Althaea officinalis contain mucilages that can aid in soothing and protecting the mucous membranes in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Dry 100g of althaea officinalis root in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 8 hours to remove moisture content.
Crush the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Mix 10g of the powder with 2g of gelatin in a small bowl to create the capsule filling.
Use a gelatin capsule machine to fill 100 capsules with the mixture prepared in step 3.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with hematuria because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. Berberine has been shown to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and kidneys, which can help alleviate symptoms of hematuria, such as blood in the urine. Additionally, goldenseal's antimicrobial properties may also help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to hematuria, further reducing its occurrence and severity. By targeting both inflammation and infection, goldenseal capsules offer a natural approach to addressing this common issue.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal contains berberine, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and combat bacterial infections that may contribute to hematuria.

Coptis: Goldenseal also contains coptis, another bioactive compound that has antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which helps to prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of hematuria such as pain and discomfort.

Berberinoids: The berberinoids present in goldenseal capsules have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the kidneys and urinary tract, ultimately reducing the frequency and severity of hematuria.
remedy_constituents: Hydrastine: This alkaloid has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the risk of infection and inflammation in the urinary tract that may cause hematuria.

Berberine: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the urinary tract from infection and oxidative damage that may contribute to hematuria.

Vasoprotectin: This compound, also known as hydrastine's vasoprotective constituent, has been shown to have a protective effect on blood vessels and may help reduce bleeding in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating hematuria symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The primary part used due to their high concentration of berberine, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that helps in treating hematuria.
Leaves: While less common, leaves are sometimes used in combination with roots and rhizomes for their additional berberine content and potential synergistic effects in treating hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Dry 50 grams of hydrastis canadensis roots for 2 hours in a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 250 milliliters of glycerin and 250 milliliters of distilled water with the powder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the mixture and seal immediately.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock capsules helps with hematuria because they contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce bleeding and discomfort associated with urinary tract issues. The plant's flavonoids also strengthen blood vessel walls, making them less prone to rupture and subsequent bleeding. Additionally, burdock root has been traditionally used to support liver function and promote the elimination of toxins from the body, which can contribute to hematuria relief. Antibacterial and Antiviral Properties: Burdock's antibacterial and antiviral properties can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to hematuria, reducing the severity and frequency of bleeding episodes.

Astringent and Anti-Edematous Effects: Burdock's astringent properties help reduce inflammation and bleeding in the urinary tract by constricting blood vessels and drying up excess mucus, while its anti-edematous effects can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hematuria.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin is a type of fructan that acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, which may help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of hematuria.

Triterpenoids (specifically, arctiin and arctigenin): Triterpenoids have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating properties, which may help protect the kidneys and urinary tract from damage and reduce the severity of hematuria.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): Flavonoids have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of hematuria.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in treating hematuria by reducing inflammation and improving urine flow.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing inflammation and protecting the kidneys from damage.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in treating hematuria by reducing inflammation and improving urine flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh arctium lappa roots and clean them thoroughly with cold water.
Dry the roots in a low temperature dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours or until completely dry.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 10 grams of the arctium lappa powder with 10 grams of cellulose powder and 5 grams of silicon dioxide powder.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with hematuria because they contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract. The herb's diuretic properties also increase urine production, which can help to flush out harmful bacteria and toxins that may be causing the bleeding. Additionally, dandelion has been shown to help heal damaged tissue in the bladder and kidneys, reducing the risk of further damage and promoting overall kidney health.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to hematuria and other urinary issues.

Choleretic and cholagogue properties: Dandelion stimulates the production of bile and gallbladder contractions, which can help to improve digestion and reduce stagnation in the digestive system that may be contributing to hematuria.

Inulin: Inulin, a type of fructan, has been shown to have diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help increase urine production and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, thereby helping to alleviate hematuria.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of taraxasterol and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate hematuria symptoms.
Flowers: Utilized because of their flavonoid and saponin content, which may contribute to their potential anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant effects in treating hematuria.
Seeds: Employed due to their rich source of inulin, a prebiotic that may aid in improving urine quality and reducing the risk of hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves from a trusted supplier or your garden.
Crush the dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Sift the taraxacum officinale powder through a 100-mesh sieve to remove any large particles.
Mix 1 gram of the sifted taraxacum officinale powder with 100 grams of gelatin powder in a small bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the taraxacum officinale mixture and seal them to preserve freshness immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with hematuria because they contain a unique blend of bioactive compounds that target the root causes of this condition. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can lead to blood in the urine. remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps with hematuria by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, which can contribute to bleeding and discomfort.
Ginkgolides: Contributes to the reduction of hematuria by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), a compound that can stimulate blood clotting and increase bleeding.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help alleviate hematuria symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the urinary tract.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba, also known as ginkgo nuts, contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, thereby alleviating hematuria symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer when they are fully ripe and dry.
Clean and dry the leaves thoroughly using a food dehydrator at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at low speed.
Mix 1 gram of the ground powder with 500 mg of gelatin powder and 500 mg of vegetable glycerin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 mg of the herbal mixture and seal them tightly using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules help with hematuria because they contain curcumin, a powerful compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When consumed orally, curcumin is absorbed into the bloodstream and reaches the kidneys where it reduces inflammation and oxidative stress caused by hematuria. This leads to a significant reduction in symptoms such as burning sensation during urination, frequent urination, and blood clots in urine. The anti-inflammatory action of curcumin also helps to prevent further damage to the urinary tract lining and promotes healthy kidney function.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory activity: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and kidneys, which can contribute to hematuria.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants, including curcumin, may help neutralize free radicals that can damage kidney tissue and cause bleeding, thereby reducing the occurrence of hematuria.

Uricosuric activity: Turmeric has been shown to increase urate excretion in the urine, which can help reduce the concentration of uric acid in the blood and prevent its deposition in kidneys, a potential cause of hematuria.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: As a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, curcumin helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, which can contribute to hematuria.
Demethoxycurcumin: This compound has been shown to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate hematuria symptoms.

Tumerone: Tumerone has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, thereby reducing the risk of hematuria.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high content of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hematuria symptoms.

Leaves: Curcuma longa leaves are used in capsule form for hematuria treatment because they contain various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

Roots: Curcuma longa roots are another commonly used part in capsules for hematuria due to their content of bioactive compounds like curcuminoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and potential anti-cancer properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry 100 grams of dried curcuma longa root in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 50 grams of dried curcuma longa root into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground powder with 100 grams of gelatin or vegetarian alternative in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the curcuma longa and gelatin mixture using a capsule filler.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedy_parts: Barks: Salix alba barks are used due to their potential diuretic properties, which help alleviate hematuria symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest salix alba leaves and stems in a sterile environment to ensure cleanliness and prevent contamination.
Dry the harvested salix alba in a temperature-controlled environment for 24 to 48 hours at 40 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried salix alba into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 200 milligrams of the ground powder with a gelatin capsule or a vegetarian alternative for encapsulation.
Fill the capsules and seal them using a capsule filling machine to ensure airtight packaging.

Grind the dried leaves and stems into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.

Dry the flowers in a dehydrator at 50c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Crush 50g of dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of the powder with 50g of gelatin and 10ml of water in a bowl.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with hematuria because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh ginger rhizome zingiber officinale roots and wash them thoroughly.

Mix 1000mg of the powdered ginger with 500mg of capsules filler and compress the mixture into capsule shells.
Store the prepared zingiber officinale capsules in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a spice grinder, yield approximately 200g.
Mix 1000mg of the ground vaccinium macrocarpon powder with 500mg of silicon dioxide as filler and 50mg of magnesium stearate as lubricant.
Fill hard-shell capsules with the mixture, 300 capsules in total, weighing approximately 400mg each.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm ventilated area for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Chop the dried achillea millefolium into small pieces and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Grind 30 grams of the chopped herbs into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the powdered herb and seal the caps using a capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley capsules helps with hematuria because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract. Hematuria is caused by damage to the lining of the bladder or kidneys, leading to bleeding. The antioxidants in parsley capsules work by neutralizing free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and irritation. This natural remedy helps soothe and protect the urinary tract, reducing hematuria symptoms and promoting overall urological health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal parsley capsules may help alleviate hematuria by reducing inflammation in the urinary tract, which can reduce bleeding and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Parsley's antioxidant compounds may help protect the kidneys from oxidative damage and repair any existing damage, which can reduce the risk of further bleeding and kidney injury.

Urinary antiseptic property: The active compounds in parsley capsules may help eliminate bacteria that can cause urinary tract infections (UTIs), which are a common underlying cause of hematuria, by inhibiting their growth and preventing infection.

Seeds: Parsley seeds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that may help protect the urinary tract from damage and reduce bleeding in cases of hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petroselinum crispum leaves at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours or until crispy.
Grind dried petroselinum crispum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix one teaspoon of the powdered herb with one tablespoon of organic coconut oil and let it infuse for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the infused mixture and seal immediately for long-term storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto capsules helps with hematuria because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which is a common underlying cause of blood in the urine. Additionally, saw palmetto may help improve bladder function and reduce symptoms of an overactive bladder, which can contribute to hematuria. remedy_properties: Serenoa repens extract: Saw palmetto's main bioactive compound, beta-sitosterol, inhibits the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to prostatitis and inflammation, which may cause hematuria.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Saw palmetto's flavonoids and fatty acids have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of hematuria such as burning or discomfort while urinating.

Antioxidant properties: Saw palmetto's antioxidant compounds, including carotenoids and polyphenols, help protect the urinary tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of hematuria.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of fatty acids and phytosterols, which have been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve urinary symptoms associated with hematuria.

Fruits: The berries of the saw palmetto plant contain compounds that may help reduce prostate size and alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), a common condition that can cause hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality serenoa repens leaves and stems, ensuring freshness and proper identity.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or machine drying method for 24 hours.
Grind dried serenoa repens into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or grinder at a dosage of 250mg per capsule.
Mix the powdered herb with 500mg of tapioca starch and 100mg of silicon dioxide for easy filling and flow.
Fill hard-shell gelatin capsules with the herbal blend using an automated capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with hematuria because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. Liquiritin and isoliquiritin: These two saponins found in licorice root have been demonstrated to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to hematuria.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and measure out 200 grams for preparation.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 500 milliliters of glycerin in a double boiler and simmer for 1 hour to create an extract.
Strain the extract through a cheesecloth or nut milk bag into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250-300 milligrams of the extracted powder and seal immediately, storing in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with hematuria because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which aid in reducing blood clotting and relaxation of blood vessels. remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various urinary tract issues, including hematuria, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Leaves: Feverfew leaves contain parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried tanacetum parthenium leaves and stems according to good manufacturing practices guidelines.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mill or grinder for at least 30 minutes.
Weigh out 250mg of the powdered herb per capsule and mix with 100mg of filler material such as silica gel.
Fill empty capsules with the herb mixture using an automatic encapsulation machine or manual capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in airtight containers at room temperature for up to 2 years or use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with hematuria because they provide anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with bleeding in the urine. remedy_properties: Emollient and demulcent properties: Slippery elm helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hematuria.

remedy_recipe: Collect fresh ulmus rubra leaves and dry them according to proper botanical methods within two days of harvesting.
Grind 20 grams of dried ulmus rubra leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Combine the powdered ulmus rubra with 50 grams of tapioca starch in a clean glass bowl, stirring well for three minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one-quarter teaspoon of the herbal mixture, sealing immediately to maintain potency within an hour.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for up to two years.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hematuria that are treated with herbal capsules are urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and bladder inflammation. Herbal remedies such as Saw Palmetto, Marshmallow Root, and Dandelion Root are used to alleviate symptoms of these conditions. These herbs help to soothe the urinary tract, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy kidney function, providing natural relief from discomfort and pain.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with hematuria is a blend of Saw Palmetto, Juniper Berries, and Marshmallow Root. Saw Palmetto helps to reduce inflammation and promote urinary tract health. Juniper Berries aid in detoxifying the kidneys and improving circulation. Marshmallow Root soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the urinary tract. This combination works synergistically to reduce bleeding, inflammation, and discomfort associated with hematuria, promoting overall urinary health and well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, herbal capsules containing extracts from Rosmarinus officinalis L. and Centaurium erythraea L. showed a diuretic effect in rats, which is beneficial in treating hematuria, a condition characterized by blood in the urine. The study involved administering the aqueous extracts of these plants to Wistar rats for 1 week, resulting in a significant enhancement of diuresis from the fifth day of treatment. The extracts, particularly at a concentration of 8% for R. officinalis, led to a peak urinary excretion of sodium, potassium, and chloride, indicating their potential in promoting the elimination of waste products and reducing the risk of hematuria.
